style="margin-top:22pt;text-align:center"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:14pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">ConocoPhillips announces first-quarter 2026 results and quarterly dividend</font></div><div id="i1441db8d205848e8870906318750346b_13"></div><div style="margin-top:22pt;padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Reported first-quarter 2026 earnings per share of $1.78 and adjusted earnings per share of $1.89. </font></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Generated cash provided by operating activities of $4.3 billion and cash from operations (CFO) of $5.4&#160;billion. </font></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Declared second-quarter ordinary dividend of $0.84 per share.</font></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Updated full-year production and capital guidance&#59; operating cost guidance unchanged.</font></div><div id="i1441db8d205848e8870906318750346b_16"></div><div style="margin-top:22pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">HOUSTON&#8212;April&#160;30, 2026&#8212;ConocoPhillips (NYSE&#58; COP) today reported first-quarter 2026 earnings of $2.2&#160;billion, or $1.78 per share, compared with first-quarter 2025 earnings of $2.8 billion, or $2.23 per share. Excluding special items, first-quarter 2026 adjusted earnings were $2.3 billion, or $1.89 per share, compared with first-quarter 2025 adjusted earnings of $2.7 billion, or $2.09 per share. </font><font style="background-color:#ffffff;color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Special items for the quarter primarily related to pending claims and settlements and a loss on a contingent liability measurement. </font></div><div><font><br></font></div><div><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:112%">&#8220;Our thoughts are with our team, partners and everyone impacted by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East,&#8221; said Ryan Lance, chairman and chief executive officer. &#8220;Amid ongoing macro volatility, ConocoPhillips delivered another quarter of strong financial and operational performance. We remain focused on delivering our value proposition&#58; operating safely&#59; maximizing our returns on and of capital, reiterating our objective to return 45% of CFO to shareholders this year&#59; and driving peer-leading free cash flow growth.&#8221;</font></div><div id="i1441db8d205848e8870906318750346b_22"></div><div style="margin-top:22pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">First-quarter highlights and recent announcements</font></div><div style="margin-top:11pt;padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Delivered total company and Lower 48 production of 2,309 thousand barrels of oil equivalent per day (MBOED) and 1,453 MBOED, respectively. </font><font style="color:#ee2724;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </font></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="background-color:#ffffff;color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="background-color:#ffffff;color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Distributed </font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">$2.0&#160;billion to shareholders, including $1.0 billion through share repurchases and $1.0&#160;billion through the ordinary dividend.</font></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Conducted successful Willow winter construction season with project achieving 50% completion.</font></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Completed four-well Alaska winter exploration program with evaluation underway and secured high-priority acreage in NPR-A lease sale.</font></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Enhanced Lower 48 capital efficiency by more than doubling percentage of 3-mile plus lateral length wells drilled compared with prior year.</font></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Executed LNG tolling agreement for third-party operated gas volumes in Equatorial Guinea, extending life of LNG facility well into the next decade.</font></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-indent:-18pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8226;</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;padding-left:12.53pt">Ended the quarter with cash and short-term investments of $6.7&#160;billion and long-term investments of $1.2 billion. </font></div><div id="i1441db8d205848e8870906318750346b_25"></div><div style="margin-top:22pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">Quarterly dividend</font></div><div style="margin-top:11pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">ConocoPhillips declared a second-quarter ordinary dividend of $0.84 per share, payable June 1, 2026,</font><font style="background-color:#ffffff;color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">to stockholders of record at the close of business on May 11, 2026. </font></div><div id="i1441db8d205848e8870906318750346b_31"></div><div style="margin-top:22pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">First-quarter review</font></div><div style="margin-top:11pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Production for the first quarter of 2026 was 2,309 MBOED, a decrease of 80 MBOED from the same period a year ago. After adjusting for closed acquisitions and dispositions, first-quarter 2026 production decreased 14 MBOED or 1% from the same period a year ago. Organic growth from Lower 48 was more than offset by downtime, which includes the impact of the Middle East conflict on Qatar, and higher Surmont royalties. </font></div><div style="height:45pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div><font><br></font></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div style="min-height:45pt;width:100%"><div><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">ConocoPhillips announces first-quarter 2026 results and quarterly dividend</font></div><div><font><br></font></div><div><font><br></font></div><div><font><br></font></div><div><font><br></font></div><div><font><br></font></div></div><div style="margin-top:11pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Lower 48 delivered production of 1,453 MBOED, including 698 MBOED from the Delaware Basin, 200 MBOED from the Midland Basin, 367 MBOED from the Eagle Ford and 183 MBOED from the Bakken. </font></div><div style="margin-top:11pt"><font style="background-color:#ffffff;color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Earnings and adjusted earnings decreased from the </font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">first</font><font style="background-color:#ffffff;color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> quarter of 2025, primarily due to lower gas prices in Permian and lower volumes, partially offset by lower costs. Earnings were further impacted by special items (see Table 1). </font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The company&#8217;s total average realized price was $50.36 per BOE, 6% lower than the $53.34 per BOE realized in the first quarter of 2025.</font></div><div style="margin-top:11pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For the quarter, cash provided by operating activities was $4.3 billion. Excluding a $1.1 billion change in operating working capital, ConocoPhillips generated CFO of $5.4 billion. The company funded $2.9 billion of capital expenditures and investments, repurchased $1.0 billion of shares, paid $1.0 billion in ordinary dividends and retired debt of $0.1 billion at maturity.</font></div><div id="i1441db8d205848e8870906318750346b_40"></div><div style="margin-top:22pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">Outlook</font></div><div><font><br></font></div><div><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">For the second quarter, the company is excluding Qatar from production guidance, given uncertainty surrounding the conflict in the Middle East. Second-quarter production is expected to be 2.185 to 2.215 million barrels of oil equivalent per day (MMBOED). </font></div><div><font><br></font></div><div><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Full-year production is expected to be 2.295 to 2.325 MMBOED. This reflects a 20 MBOED annual adjustment for Qatar, given the exclusion of Qatar production from second-quarter guidance, as well as a 15 MBOED annual royalty rate adjustment at Surmont due to higher oil prices.</font></div><div><font><br></font></div><div><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Capital spending for 2026 is expected to be $12 to $12.5 billion, including incremental Permian activity. The range reflects uncertainty around the macro environment and North Field East and North Field South capital timing in Qatar.</font></div><div id="i1441db8d205848e8870906318750346b_43"></div><div style="margin-top:22pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">ConocoPhillips will host a conference call today at 12&#58;00 p.m. Eastern time to discuss this announcement. To listen to the call and view related presentation materials and supplemental information, go to </font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">www.conocophillips.com&#47;investor</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. A recording and transcript of the call will be posted afterward.</font></div><div style="margin-top:11pt;text-align:center"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">--- # # # ---</font></div><div style="margin-top:11pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline">About ConocoPhillips</font></div><div style="margin-top:11pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:11pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As a leading global exploration and production company, ConocoPhillips is uniquely equipped to deliver reliable, responsibly produced oil and gas. Our deep, durable and diverse portfolio is built to meet growing global energy demands. Together with our high-performing operations and continuously advancing technology, we are well positioned to deliver strong, consistent financial results, now and for decades to come. Visit us at www.conocophillips.com. </font></div><div id="i1441db8d205848e8870906318750346b_46"></div><div style="margin-top:22pt"><font global and regional changes in the demand, supply, prices, differentials or other market conditions affecting oil and gas, including changes as a result of any ongoing military conflict and the global response to such conflict, security threats on facilities and infrastructure, global health crises, the imposition or lifting of crude oil production quotas or other actions that might be imposed by OPEC and other producing countries or the resulting company or third-party actions in response to such changes&#59; the potential for insufficient liquidity or other factors that could impact our ability to repurchase shares and declare and pay dividends&#59; potential failures or delays in achieving expected reserve or production levels from existing and future oil and gas developments, including due to operating hazards, drilling risks and the inherent uncertainties in predicting reserves and reservoir performance&#59; reductions in our reserve replacement rates, whether as a result of significant declines in commodity prices or otherwise&#59; unsuccessful exploratory drilling activities or the inability to obtain access to exploratory acreage&#59; failure to progress or complete announced and future development plans related to constructing, modifying or operating E&#38;P and LNG facilities, or unexpected changes in costs, inflationary pressures or technical equipment related to such plans&#59; significant operational or investment changes imposed by legislative and regulatory initiatives and international agreements addressing environmental concerns, including initiatives addressing the impact of global climate change, such as limiting or reducing GHG emissions, regulations concerning hydraulic fracturing, methane emissions, flaring or water disposal and prohibitions on commodity exports&#59; substantial investment in and increased adoption of competing or alternative energy sources&#59; risks, uncertainties and high costs that may prevent us from successfully executing on our Climate-related Risk Strategy&#59; lack or inadequacy of, or disruptions in reliable transportation for our crude oil, bitumen, natural gas, LNG and NGLs&#59; inability to timely obtain or maintain permits, including those necessary for construction, drilling and&#47;or development, or inability to make capital expenditures required to maintain compliance with any necessary permits or applicable laws or regulations&#59; potential disruption or interruption of our operations and any resulting consequences due to accidents, extraordinary weather events, supply chain disruptions, civil unrest, political events, war, terrorism, cybersecurity threats or information technology failures, constraints or disruptions&#59; liability for remedial actions, including removal and reclamation obligations, under existing or future environmental regulations and litigation&#59; liability resulting from pending or future litigation or our failure to comply with applicable laws and regulations&#59; general domestic and international economic, political and diplomatic developments, including deterioration of international trade relationships, the imposition of trade restrictions or tariffs relating to commodities and material or products (such as aluminum and steel) used in the operation of our business, expropriation of assets, changes in governmental policies relating to commodity pricing, including the imposition of price caps, sanctions or other adverse regulations or taxation policies&#59; competition and consolidation in the oil and gas E&#38;P industry, including competition for sources of supply, services, personnel and equipment&#59; any limitations on our access to capital or increase in our cost of capital or insurance, including as a result of illiquidity, changes or uncertainty in domestic or international financial markets, foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations or investment sentiment&#59; challenges or delays to our execution of, or successful implementation of any asset dispositions or acquisitions we elect to pursue&#59; potential disruption of our operations, including the diversion of management time and attention&#59; our inability to realize anticipated cost savings or capital expenditure reductions&#59; difficulties integrating acquired businesses and technologies&#59; or other unanticipated changes&#59; our inability to deploy the net proceeds from any asset dispositions that are pending or that we elect to undertake in the future in the manner and timeframe we anticipate, if at all&#59; the operation, financing and management of risks of our joint ventures&#59; the ability of our customers and other contractual counterparties to satisfy their obligations to us, including our ability to collect payments when due from the government of Venezuela or PDVSA&#59; uncertainty as to the long-term value of our common stock&#59; and other economic, business, competitive and&#47;or regulatory factors affecting our business generally as set forth in our filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Unless legally required, ConocoPhillips expressly disclaims any obligation to update any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise. </font></div><div style="margin-top:9pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Cautionary Note to U.S. Investors </font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8211; The SEC permits oil and gas companies, in their filings with the SEC, to disclose only proved, probable and possible reserves. We may use the term &#8220;resource&#8221; in this news release that the SEC&#8217;s guidelines prohibit us from including in filings with the SEC. U.S. investors are urged to consider closely the oil and gas disclosures in our Form 10-K and other reports and filings with the SEC. Copies are available from the SEC and from the ConocoPhillips website.</font></div><div style="margin-top:9pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Use of Non-GAAP Financial Information</font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> &#8211; To supplement the presentation of the company&#8217;s financial results prepared in accordance with U.S. generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP), this news release and the accompanying supplemental financial information contain certain financial measures that are not prepared in accordance with GAAP, including adjusted earnings (calculated on a consolidated and on a segment-level basis), adjusted earnings per share (EPS), free cash flow (FCF)</font><font style="color:#ee2724;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">and</font><font style="color:#ee2724;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> </font><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">cash from operations (CFO).</font></div><div style="margin-top:9pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The company believes that the non-GAAP measure adjusted earnings (both on an aggregate and a per-share basis) is useful to investors to help facilitate comparisons of the company&#8217;s operating performance associated with the company&#8217;s core business operations across periods on a consistent basis and with the performance and cost structures of peer companies by excluding items that do not directly relate to the company&#8217;s core business operations. Adjusted earnings is defined as earnings removing the impact of special items. </font></div><div style="height:45pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div><font><br></font></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div style="min-height:45pt;width:100%"><div><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">ConocoPhillips announces first-quarter 2026 results and quarterly dividend</font></div><div><font><br></font></div></div><div style="margin-top:9pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Adjusted EPS is a measure of the company&#8217;s diluted net earnings per share excluding special items. The company further believes that the non-GAAP measure CFO is useful to investors to help understand changes in cash provided by operating activities excluding the timing effects associated with operating working capital changes across periods on a consistent basis and for comparison with the performance of peer companies. The company believes that the above-mentioned non-GAAP measures, when viewed in combination with the company&#8217;s results prepared in accordance with GAAP, provides a more complete understanding of the factors and trends affecting the company&#8217;s business and performance. The company&#8217;s Board of Directors and management also use these non-GAAP measures to analyze the company&#8217;s operating performance across periods when overseeing and managing the company&#8217;s business. </font></div><div style="margin-top:9pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Each of the non-GAAP measures included in this news release and the accompanying supplemental financial information has limitations as an analytical tool and should not be considered in isolation or as a substitute for an analysis of the company&#8217;s results calculated in accordance with GAAP. In addition, because not all companies use identical calculations, the company&#8217;s presentation of non-GAAP measures in this news release and the accompanying supplemental financial information may not be comparable to similarly titled measures disclosed by other companies, including companies in our industry. The company may also change the calculation of any of the non-GAAP measures included in this news release and the accompanying supplemental financial information from time to time in light of its then existing operations to include other adjustments that may impact its operations.</font></div><div style="margin-top:9pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Reconciliations of each non-GAAP measure presented in this news release to the most directly comparable financial measure calculated in accordance with GAAP are included in the release.</font></div><div style="margin-top:9pt"><font style="color:#000000;font-family:'Calibri',sans-serif;font-size:9pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Other Terms &#8211; This news release also may contain the term pro forma underlying production. Pro forma underlying production reflects the impact of closed acquisitions and closed dispositions as of March&#160;31, 2026. The impact of closed acquisitions and dispositions assumes a closing date of January 1, 2025. The company believes that underlying production is useful to investors to compare production reflecting the impact of closed acquisitions and dispositions on a consistent go-forward basis across periods and with peer companies. Return of capital is defined as the total of the ordinary dividend and share repurchases. References in the release to project capital exclude capitalized interest and references to earnings refer to net income. </font></div><div style="height:45pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div
